Interface TypeAdapter<T>

Type Parameters:
T - type of the adapter
public interface TypeAdapter<T>
Interface used to (de-)serialize custom types with StandardSerializer.

Please note that classes implementing this interface must also be registered to the used serializer instance. See StandardSerializer.register(Class, TypeAdapter).

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    T
    deserialize(Map<Object,Object> map)
    Deserializes the given map into instance of this type.
    Map<Object,Object>
    serialize(T object)
    Serializes the given instance into a map.
    default Map<String,Object>
    toStringKeyedMap(Map<?,?> map)
    Converts this map (including all sub-maps) to String=value HashMap.

  • Method Details

    • serialize

      @NotNull Map<Object,Object> serialize(@NotNull T object)
      Serializes the given instance into a map.

      The returned map does not need to (but may) contain the type identifier: one entry in the top-level map (the one returned), where the key is defined by the serializer (== for StandardSerializer.getDefault()) and the value identifies the serialized type - either by the full canonical classname (e.g. me.name.project.objects.CustomObject) or it's alias. If you decide to include a type identifier, it must be registered (this requirement is not verified).

      If the returned map does not contain the identifier, the serializer will automatically use the full classname.

      Parameters:
      object - object to serialize
      Returns:
      the serialized object

    • deserialize

      @NotNull T deserialize(@NotNull Map<Object,Object> map)
      Deserializes the given map into instance of this type.

      The given map is a raw object map; there are no blocks, just native Java objects themselves.

      Use toStringKeyedMap(Map) to convert the map.

      Parameters:
      map - the raw map to deserialize
      Returns:
      the deserialized object

    • toStringKeyedMap

      @NotNull default Map<String,Object> toStringKeyedMap(@NotNull Map<?,?> map)
      Converts this map (including all sub-maps) to String=value HashMap.
      Parameters:
      map - the map to convert
      Returns:
      the converted map
